perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of FRANKLIN, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and any and all foreign countries, on said inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, without further consideration.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of FRANKLIN, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and any and all foreign countries, on said inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, without further consideration, except for inventions the EMPLOYEE chooses to retain rights to, as per the conditions stipulated in Section 1.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction with Section 1, which now allows the employee to retain rights to certain inventions. This directly contradicts the requirement to assign 'all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee'. The added exception makes it unclear whether the employee is obligated to assign all inventions as initially stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of FRANKLIN, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and any and all foreign countries, on said inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, without further consideration, except for inventions the EMPLOYEE chooses to retain rights to, as per the conditions stipulated in Section 1.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception allowing the employee to retain rights to certain inventions, as stipulated in Section 1. The contradicted text states that the employee shall assign and transfer all applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN. The addition of the exception in the changed text directly contradicts the 'all' assignment requirement in the contradicted text, thus creating an in-text contradiction according to the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request of FRANKLIN execute all papers and do all other things that may be reasonably required in order to protect the rights of FRANKLIN, and to vest in FRANKLIN or its successors or assigns the entire rights, title and interest in and to any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, program improvements, and applications for letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations relating to anything pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN as provided above.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request of FRANKLIN execute all papers and do all other things that may be reasonably required in order to protect the rights of FRANKLIN, and to vest in FRANKLIN or its successors or assigns the rights, title and interest in and to any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, program improvements, and applications for letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations relating to anything pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN as provided above, unless those rights are specifically retained by the employee as per the stipulations outlined in Section 1.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'unless those rights are specifically retained by the employee as per the stipulations outlined in Section 1' contradicts the original commitment to vest 'the entire rights, title and interest' in FRANKLIN. It creates ambiguity about which rights the employee is obligated to transfer, contradicting the encompassing nature of the initial obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request of FRANKLIN execute all papers and do all other things that may be reasonably required in order to protect the rights of FRANKLIN, and to vest in FRANKLIN or its successors or assigns the rights, title and interest in and to any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, program improvements, and applications for letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations relating to anything pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN as provided above, unless those rights are specifically retained by the employee as per the stipulations outlined in Section 1.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('unless those rights are specifically retained by the employee as per the stipulations outlined in Section 1') to the original obligation of the employee to vest 'the entire rights, title and interest' in FRANKLIN. This creates a direct contradiction within the document, as it is no longer clear which rights the employee is obligated to transfer, thus making the previous commitment uncertain. The changed and contradicted text are in the same location.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE further agrees not to divulge to any third party, either during his or her employment or thereafter, any confidential information conceived by him or her, disclosed by FRANKLIN, or obtained by him or her while in the employment of FRANKLIN relating in any way to any of FRANKLIN'S processes, businesses, customers, trade secrets, apparatus, products, software, packages, programs or trends in research, or to any of the inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements covered hereby, and agrees to maintain this information in confidence until such time that such information has become widely known to the public or described in an issued patent or in a printed publication of wide circulation. Upon termination of the employment, EMPLOYEE agrees to turn over to FRANKLIN all notes, memoranda, notebooks, drawings, records, customer lists, telephone files (including Rolodex and business card files) and correspondence in connection with anything done by him or her relating to his or her employment, for the reason that all confidential information contained therein is at all times the sole property of FRANKLIN. Following the termination of his or her employment with FRANKLIN, EMPLOYEE agrees not to solicit any employees or consultants of Franklin at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE further agrees not to divulge to any third party, either during his or her employment or thereafter, any confidential information conceived by him or her, disclosed by FRANKLIN, or obtained by him or her while in the employment of FRANKLIN relating in any way to any of FRANKLIN'S processes, businesses, customers, trade secrets, apparatus, products, software, packages, programs or trends in research, or to any of the inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements covered hereby, and agrees to maintain this information in confidence until such time that such information has become widely known to the public or described in an issued patent or in a printed publication of wide circulation, except for confidential information related to inventions or improvements that the EMPLOYEE retains ownership of as defined in Section 1. Upon termination of the employment, EMPLOYEE agrees to turn over to FRANKLIN all notes, memoranda, notebooks, drawings, records, customer lists, telephone files (including Rolodex and business card files) and correspondence in connection with anything done by him or her relating to his or her employment, for the reason that all confidential information contained therein is at all times the sole property of FRANKLIN. Following the termination of his or her employment with FRANKLIN, EMPLOYEE agrees not to solicit any employees or consultants of Franklin at any time.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except for confidential information related to inventions or improvements that the EMPLOYEE retains ownership of as defined in Section 1' a contradiction is introduced. It suggests that the employee is not obligated to keep confidential information relating to inventions they own, potentially conflicting with the earlier statement about maintaining 'all' confidential information and directly creates and in-text contradiction regarding the employee´s obligation to keep everything confidential. In that case it should be stated that ´all confidential information contained therein is at all times the sole property of FRANKLIN´.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE further agrees not to divulge to any third party, either during his or her employment or thereafter, any confidential information conceived by him or her, disclosed by FRANKLIN, or obtained by him or her while in the employment of FRANKLIN relating in any way to any of FRANKLIN'S processes, businesses, customers, trade secrets, apparatus, products, software, packages, programs or trends in research, or to any of the inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements covered hereby, and agrees to maintain this information in confidence until such time that such information has become widely known to the public or described in an issued patent or in a printed publication of wide circulation, except for confidential information related to inventions or improvements that the EMPLOYEE retains ownership of as defined in Section 1. Upon termination of the employment, EMPLOYEE agrees to turn over to FRANKLIN all notes, memoranda, notebooks, drawings, records, customer lists, telephone files (including Rolodex and business card files) and correspondence in connection with anything done by him or her relating to his or her employment, for the reason that all confidential information contained therein is at all times the sole property of FRANKLIN. Following the termination of his or her employment with FRANKLIN, EMPLOYEE agrees not to solicit any employees or consultants of Franklin at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception ('except for confidential information related to inventions or improvements that the EMPLOYEE retains ownership of as defined in Section 1') to the original blanket statement regarding the employee's obligation to maintain all confidential information. This directly contradicts the later part of the clause ('all confidential information contained therein is at all times the sole property of FRANKLIN'). The employee is now potentially allowed to divulge confidential information related to their owned inventions, creating a clear in-text contradiction about what constitutes confidential information and who owns it.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE understands and agrees that the continuance of EMPLOYEE in the service of FRANKLIN for a definite period is not made obligatory upon either party or a condition of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE understands and agrees that the continuance of EMPLOYEE in the service of FRANKLIN for a definite period is not made obligatory upon either party or a condition of this Agreement, unless otherwise stipulated in a separate employment contract.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase, 'unless otherwise stipulated in a separate employment contract,' introduces a condition that contradicts the initial statement that employment is not obligatory. If a separate contract exists that *does* obligate the employee for a specific period, it conflicts with the blanket statement in this clause, thus creating an internal contradiction. This creates uncertainty about which agreement controls employment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE understands and agrees that the continuance of EMPLOYEE in the service of FRANKLIN for a definite period is not made obligatory upon either party or a condition of this Agreement, unless otherwise stipulated in a separate employment contract.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('unless otherwise stipulated in a separate employment contract') to the original statement that employment is not obligatory. This directly contradicts the initial assertion, as a separate contract *could* impose an obligation. This falls under 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' because it creates uncertainty and makes interpretation unclear, since two agreements are now in play potentially saying different things.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE agrees that any portion of this Agreement held to be unenforceable shall be severed from this Agreement and shall not adversely affect any other portion of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE agrees that any portion of this Agreement held to be unenforceable shall be severed from this Agreement and shall not adversely affect any other portion of this Agreement, except in cases where the severed portion is deemed material to the overall intent of the agreement, as determined by FRANKLIN.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'except in cases where the severed portion is deemed material to the overall intent of the agreement, as determined by FRANKLIN' introduces a contradiction. The original clause states that severance will *not* adversely affect any other portion, while the added exception allows FRANKLIN to determine that it *can* adversely affect the agreement if deemed material. This creates an in-text contradiction and ambiguity, as it depends on FRANKLIN's interpretation, and creating a significant vulnerability for the EMPLOYEE.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE agrees that any portion of this Agreement held to be unenforceable shall be severed from this Agreement and shall not adversely affect any other portion of this Agreement, except in cases where the severed portion is deemed material to the overall intent of the agreement, as determined by FRANKLIN.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the original clause regarding severance. The original text states that severance will not adversely affect any other portion of the agreement, while the changed text allows FRANKLIN to determine that it can adversely affect the agreement if deemed material. This directly contradicts the original statement, creating a clear and impactful in-text contradiction.
